summary: Frontend FAILS TO COMPILE due to a critical syntax error in /app/frontend/src/App.js. The CheckoutModal component definition (starting line 266) is broken: it begins defining a Zod schema with `const schema = z.object({` at line 269, but immediately after that line, the entire FeaturesSection and FAQSection components are pasted in the middle of the unterminated object literal. The schema fields (name/email/phone/...) only appear at line 391, far below FeaturesSection/FAQSection. Webpack throws: `Line 269: Parsing error: Unexpected keyword 'const'. (269:0)`. Because the bundle never compiles, the landing page does not render at all in the browser, and no UI flows (sections rendering, checkout, mock payment, chatbot) can be tested. Backend code review was performed (see comments).

minor.issue: Model name confusion: code first calls .with_model('gemini', 'gemini-3-flash-preview') with an inline comment expressing uncertainty, then immediately overrides with .with_model('gemini', 'gemini-2.5-flash'). The first call should simply be removed. Also chat history is not actually sent to LLM — only the last message is forwarded as UserMessage, so multi-turn context is lost.

No signature verification (mocked Duitku) — any caller can mark any order as paid by POSTing {merchantOrderId, resultCode:'00'}. Acceptable for MOCK but must be flagged. Also `master_slots_collection.usedSlots` is incremented every time the callback is hit for a master order — no idempotency check (status already 'paid' is not guarded), so repeated callbacks will over-count slots.
paymentUrl built from NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL env var. This is fine, but a Next.js-style env name in a FastAPI/CRA app is misleading — rename to PUBLIC_BASE_URL or FRONTEND_URL.
@app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in modern FastAPI — use lifespan handlers.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL SYNTAX ERROR — CheckoutModal's Zod schema definition is split apart by FeaturesSection and FAQSection components inserted in the middle of `z.object({ ... })`. Webpack parse error at line 269 ('Unexpected keyword const'). Bundle fails to compile, app does not render.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot test any flow (landing render, checkout modal, mock payment, success page, chatbot) because frontend won't compile.

action_items: URGENT: Restructure /app/frontend/src/App.js — move FeaturesSection (currently lines 269-294) and FAQSection (currently lines 361-390) OUT of the CheckoutModal function body. CheckoutModal must contain a single contiguous block: `const schema = z.object({ name:..., email:..., phone:..., institution:..., job:... });` followed by useForm, onSubmit, and the return JSX. After restructuring, run `yarn build` or check supervisor frontend log to confirm no parse errors.
After fix compiles: re-run E2E test for landing sections, checkout (skill 199000) -> /mock-payment -> simulate pay -> /payment-success, and chatbot send/receive.
Backend: remove duplicate .with_model() call in /api/chat and pass full message history to LlmChat (or document single-turn intentionally).
Backend: add idempotency guard in /api/payment-callback — only update status and increment usedSlots when current status != 'paid'.
Rename NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL env var to a framework-neutral name (e.g., PUBLIC_BASE_URL).
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is 730 lines — split into /components and /pages once syntax is fixed.

Mocked APIs: Duitku checkout (returns local /mock-payment URL) and Nodemailer email dispatch (only a print statement in /api/payment-callback) — both clearly labeled MOCKED. No real email is sent; the success page tells the user to check their inbox which will be empty.
/api/payment-callback lacks idempotency — master slot counter can be inflated by repeated callbacks.
Chat endpoint silently drops conversation history; only last message is sent to Gemini.

No data-testid on the landing-page section wrappers (Hero, Problems, IRDR, FAQ, Testimonials, WebinarPromo) — only on buttons/inputs. Adding section-level test ids would simplify section-rendering assertions.
Frontend has no global error boundary; any runtime error blanks the page.

success_rate.backend: Not measured — pytest not executed because primary task is blocked by frontend compile failure; backend code review only.

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent restructures App.js so CheckoutModal/FeaturesSection/FAQSection are separate top-level components, verify webpack compiles cleanly (check /var/log/supervisor/frontend.out.log). Then perform: (1) GET / and assert each section renders, (2) click [data-testid=buy-skill] -> fill form -> [data-testid=submit-checkout] -> expect navigation to /mock-payment?orderId=... , (3) click [data-testid=mock-pay-btn] -> expect navigation to /payment-success and [data-testid=success-home-btn] visible, (4) open chatbot via [data-testid=toggle-chat], send a message via [data-testid=chat-input]+[data-testid=chat-send], wait ~10s for Gemini reply. Also write pytest covering /api/slots, /api/create-payment, /api/payment-callback (with idempotency), /api/download/{token}, /api/chat.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: developer pasted FeaturesSection and FAQSection component definitions into the middle of CheckoutModal's body, immediately after `const schema = z.object({` (line 269), instead of placing them as siblings before/after CheckoutModal. The trailing schema property list (lines 391-396) is then floating in module scope, also a syntax error. Reproduction: `cat /app/frontend/src/App.js | head -n 400 | tail -n 140` shows the splice. Mitigation: cut lines 269-390 (FeaturesSection through end of FAQSection) and paste them above the `const CheckoutModal = ...` declaration, then verify lines 391-396 ({ name, email, phone, institution, job }) immediately follow `const schema = z.object({` inside CheckoutModal.
